title: Exercises: Thermal Management
description: Practice thermal network analysis, Kapitza resistance calculations, and thermalization time estimation

exercises.title: Kapitza Resistance Calculator
exercises.description: Implement a tool to analyze Kapitza resistance and its impact on cooling in dilution refrigerator mixing chambers. Calculate how sintered surface area requirements scale with temperature.
exercises.starterCode:

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def kapitza_resistance(T: float, area_cm2: float, A_K: float = 0.02) -> float:
    """
    Calculate Kapitza thermal resistance.
    
    Args:
        T: Temperature in Kelvin

    Returns:
        Required surface area in cm^2

def sintered_powder_area(volume_cm3: float, particle_diameter_um: float) -> float:

    # Assume spherical particles and 60% packing density

# Test your implementation
print("Kapitza Resistance Analysis")
print("="*50)

# Calculate R_K at different temperatures
for T in [0.010, 0.015, 0.020, 0.050, 0.100]:
    R = kapitza_resistance(T, area_cm2=100)
    print(f"T = {T*1000:.0f} mK, Area = 100 cm²: R_K = {R:.1f} K/W")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def kapitza_resistance(T: float, area_cm2: float, A_K: float = 0.02) -> float:
    """
    Calculate Kapitza thermal resistance.
    R_K = A_K / (T^3 * Area)
    """
    if T <= 0 or area_cm2 <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Temperature and area must be positive")
    return A_K / (T**3 * area_cm2)

def required_surface_area(T: float, Q_max: float, dT_max: float, 
                          A_K: float = 0.02) -> float:
    """
    Calculate required surface area to limit temperature rise.
    
    From R_K = dT/Q = A_K/(T^3 * A)
    Solving: A = A_K * Q_max / (T^3 * dT_max)
    """
    return A_K * Q_max / (T**3 * dT_max)

def sintered_powder_area(volume_cm3: float, particle_diameter_um: float) -> float:
    """
    Estimate surface area of sintered metal powder.
    Assuming spherical particles and 60% packing density.
    """
    # Convert particle diameter to cm
    d_cm = particle_diameter_um * 1e-4
    
    # Volume and surface area of single sphere
    V_sphere = (4/3) * np.pi * (d_cm/2)**3
    A_sphere = 4 * np.pi * (d_cm/2)**2
    
    # Effective solid volume (60% packing)
    packing_density = 0.6
    solid_volume = volume_cm3 * packing_density
    
    # Number of spheres
    n_spheres = solid_volume / V_sphere
    
    # Total surface area (accounting for ~30% contact area loss)
    contact_factor = 0.7
    total_area = n_spheres * A_sphere * contact_factor
    
    return total_area

# Test implementation
print("Kapitza Resistance Analysis")
print("="*60)

# Part 1: R_K at different temperatures
print("\n1. Kapitza Resistance vs Temperature (100 cm² surface):")
print("-"*60)
for T in [0.010, 0.015, 0.020, 0.050, 0.100]:
    R = kapitza_resistance(T, area_cm2=100)
    print(f"   T = {T*1000:5.0f} mK: R_K = {R:10.1f} K/W")

# Part 2: Required area for mixing chamber
print("\n2. Required Surface Area for 10 uW load, 1 mK rise:")
print("-"*60)
Q_load = 10e-6  # 10 uW
dT_max = 0.001  # 1 mK

for T in [0.010, 0.015, 0.020, 0.050]:
    A_req = required_surface_area(T, Q_load, dT_max)
    print(f"   T = {T*1000:5.0f} mK: Required area = {A_req:.0f} cm²")

# Part 3: Sintered powder design
print("\n3. Sintered Silver Powder Surface Area:")
print("-"*60)
for d_um in [1, 5, 10, 50]:
    A_sinter = sintered_powder_area(volume_cm3=1, particle_diameter_um=d_um)
    print(f"   Particle d = {d_um:2d} um, 1 cm³: Area = {A_sinter:.0f} cm²")

# Part 4: Complete mixing chamber design
print("\n4. Mixing Chamber Heat Exchanger Design:")
print("-"*60)
T_target = 0.015  # 15 mK
Q_target = 15e-6  # 15 uW cooling power
dT_allowed = 0.002  # 2 mK temperature drop allowed

A_required = required_surface_area(T_target, Q_target, dT_allowed)
print(f"   Target: {Q_target*1e6:.0f} uW at {T_target*1000:.0f} mK")
print(f"   Max dT: {dT_allowed*1000:.0f} mK")
print(f"   Required area: {A_required:.0f} cm²")

# Design with 5 um silver particles
d_particle = 5  # um
V_needed = A_required / sintered_powder_area(1, d_particle)
print(f"\n   Using 5 um silver particles:")
print(f"   Volume needed: {V_needed:.2f} cm³")
print(f"   (~{V_needed**(1/3)*10:.0f} mm cube equivalent)")

# Plot
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# Plot 1: R_K vs temperature
T_range = np.linspace(0.005, 0.2, 100)
for area in [10, 100, 1000]:
    R_values = [kapitza_resistance(T, area) for T in T_range]
    axes[0].loglog(T_range*1000, R_values, linewidth=2, label=f'{area} cm²')

axes[0].set_xlabel('Temperature (mK)')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Kapitza Resistance (K/W)')
axes[0].set_title('Kapitza Resistance vs Temperature')
axes[0].legend(title='Surface Area')
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3, which='both')

# Plot 2: Required area vs temperature
for Q in [1e-6, 10e-6, 100e-6]:
    A_values = [required_surface_area(T, Q, 0.001) for T in T_range]
    axes[1].loglog(T_range*1000, A_values, linewidth=2, 
                   label=f'{Q*1e6:.0f} uW')

axes[1].set_xlabel('Temperature (mK)')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Required Area (cm²)')
axes[1].set_title('Required Area for 1 mK Rise')
axes[1].legend(title='Heat Load')
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3, which='both')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
hints: Rearrange to solve for required area: A = A_K * Q / (T^3 * dT)
Sintered powder: calculate number of spheres and multiply by surface area per sphere

exercises.title: Thermal Network Solver
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive thermal network solver that can analyze complex heat flow paths in cryogenic systems. Include support for series/parallel combinations, temperature-dependent conductivities, and thermal capacitances.

# Test with sample mounting problem
network = ThermalNetworkSolver()

# Add nodes
network.add_node('MC', T_initial=0.015)
network.add_node('holder')

# Add elements (with temperature dependence for Kapitza)

# Connect

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple, Optional

@dataclass
class ThermalElement:
    """A thermal resistance element with optional T dependence."""
    name: str
    R_base: float
    T_ref: float = 1.0
    T_exponent: float = 0.0
    
    def resistance_at_T(self, T: float) -> float:
        """Calculate resistance at given temperature."""
        if self.T_exponent == 0:
            return self.R_base
        return self.R_base * (T / self.T_ref) ** self.T_exponent

class ThermalNetworkSolver:
    """
    Solve complex thermal networks with temperature-dependent properties.
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.nodes: Dict[str, float] = {}
        self.elements: Dict[str, ThermalElement] = {}
        self.connections: List[Tuple[str, str, str]] = []
        self.boundary_temps: Dict[str, float] = {}
        self.heat_loads: Dict[str, float] = {}
    
    def add_node(self, name: str, T_initial: float = 0.020):
        """Add a node to the network."""
        self.nodes[name] = T_initial
    
    def add_element(self, name: str, R: float, T_exponent: float = 0.0):
        """Add a thermal element."""
        self.elements[name] = ThermalElement(
            name=name, R_base=R, T_ref=1.0, T_exponent=T_exponent
        )
    
    def connect(self, node1: str, node2: str, element: str):
        """Connect two nodes with a thermal element."""
        if node1 not in self.nodes:
            self.add_node(node1)
        if node2 not in self.nodes:
            self.add_node(node2)
        self.connections.append((node1, node2, element))
    
    def set_boundary_temperature(self, node: str, T: float):
        """Fix a node at a constant temperature."""
        self.boundary_temps[node] = T
        self.nodes[node] = T
    
    def add_heat_load(self, node: str, Q: float):
        """Add a heat load to a node (positive = heat added)."""
        self.heat_loads[node] = self.heat_loads.get(node, 0) + Q
    
    def _get_element_R(self, element_name: str, T: float) -> float:
        """Get resistance of element at temperature T."""
        elem = self.elements[element_name]
        return elem.resistance_at_T(T)
    
    def solve(self, tolerance: float = 1e-9, max_iterations: int = 200) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """
        Solve for steady-state temperatures using iterative method.
        """
        # Initialize non-boundary nodes
        for node in self.nodes:
            if node not in self.boundary_temps:
                # Set initial guess based on nearest boundary
                self.nodes[node] = 0.020
        
        # Apply boundary temperatures
        for node, T in self.boundary_temps.items():
            self.nodes[node] = T
        
        # Iterative solution
        for iteration in range(max_iterations):
            max_change = 0
            
            for node in self.nodes:
                if node in self.boundary_temps:
                    continue  # Skip fixed temperature nodes
                
                # Sum of conductances and weighted temperatures
                sum_G = 0
                sum_GT = 0
                
                for (n1, n2, elem_name) in self.connections:
                    if node == n1:
                        other_node = n2
                    elif node == n2:
                        other_node = n1
                    else:
                        continue
                    
                    # Get temperature-dependent resistance
                    T_avg = (self.nodes[node] + self.nodes[other_node]) / 2
                    R = self._get_element_R(elem_name, T_avg)
                    G = 1 / R
                    
                    sum_G += G
                    sum_GT += G * self.nodes[other_node]
                
                # Include heat load
                Q = self.heat_loads.get(node, 0)
                
                if sum_G > 0:
                    T_new = (sum_GT + Q) / sum_G
                    
                    # Track convergence
                    change = abs(T_new - self.nodes[node])
                    max_change = max(max_change, change)
                    
                    # Under-relaxation for stability
                    self.nodes[node] = 0.7 * T_new + 0.3 * self.nodes[node]
            
            if max_change < tolerance:
                print(f"Converged after {iteration+1} iterations")
                break
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Did not converge after {max_iterations} iterations")
        
        return self.nodes.copy()
    
    def get_heat_flows(self) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Calculate heat flow through each element."""
        flows = {}
        
        for (n1, n2, elem_name) in self.connections:
            T1, T2 = self.nodes[n1], self.nodes[n2]
            T_avg = (T1 + T2) / 2
            R = self._get_element_R(elem_name, T_avg)
            Q = (T1 - T2) / R  # Positive if T1 > T2
            flows[elem_name] = Q
        
        return flows
    
    def print_summary(self):
        """Print a summary of the solved network."""
        print("\nThermal Network Solution Summary")
        print("="*60)
        
        print("\nNode Temperatures:")
        for node, T in sorted(self.nodes.items()):
            marker = " (fixed)" if node in self.boundary_temps else ""
            print(f"  {node}: {T*1000:.3f} mK{marker}")
        
        print("\nHeat Flows:")
        flows = self.get_heat_flows()
        for elem, Q in flows.items():
            print(f"  {elem}: {Q*1e9:.3f} nW")
        
        print("\nHeat Loads Applied:")
        for node, Q in self.heat_loads.items():
            print(f"  {node}: {Q*1e9:.3f} nW")

# Test with sample mounting problem
print("Sample Mounting Thermal Analysis")
print("="*60)

network = ThermalNetworkSolver()

# Add elements with temperature dependence
network.add_element('kapitza_mc', R=50, T_exponent=-3)  # Kapitza: R ~ T^-3
network.add_element('holder_conduction', R=10, T_exponent=-1)  # Cu: R ~ T^-1 approx
network.add_element('sample_contact', R=30, T_exponent=0)  # Contact: ~constant

# Build network
network.connect('MC', 'holder', 'kapitza_mc')
network.connect('holder', 'sample', 'holder_conduction')
network.connect('holder', 'sample', 'sample_contact')  # Parallel path

# Set boundary and loads
network.set_boundary_temperature('MC', 0.015)  # 15 mK mixing chamber
network.add_heat_load('sample', 1e-9)  # 1 nW sample dissipation

# Solve and display
temperatures = network.solve()
network.print_summary()

# Parametric study: vary dissipation
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("Parametric Study: Sample Temperature vs Dissipation")
print("-"*60)

for Q_nW in [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0]:
    net = ThermalNetworkSolver()
    net.add_element('kapitza_mc', R=50, T_exponent=-3)
    net.add_element('holder_conduction', R=10, T_exponent=-1)
    net.add_element('sample_contact', R=30, T_exponent=0)
    net.connect('MC', 'holder', 'kapitza_mc')
    net.connect('holder', 'sample', 'holder_conduction')
    net.connect('holder', 'sample', 'sample_contact')
    net.set_boundary_temperature('MC', 0.015)
    net.add_heat_load('sample', Q_nW * 1e-9)
    temps = net.solve()
    dT = (temps['sample'] - 0.015) * 1000
    print(f"  Q = {Q_nW:5.1f} nW: T_sample = {temps['sample']*1000:.2f} mK (dT = {dT:.2f} mK)")
hints: Use iterative solution for temperature-dependent resistances
Apply energy balance: sum of heat flows = heat load at each node
For convergence, use under-relaxation (mix new and old temperatures)

testCases.expected: Sample temperature ~ 16-18 mK depending on resistances

exercises.title: Thermalization Time Analysis
exercises.description:
A gold wire bond (25 um diameter, 2 mm long) connects a qubit chip to its carrier. Analyze the thermalization properties:

a) Calculate the thermal resistance of the wire bond at 20 mK
b) Estimate the heat capacity of the wire (electronic + phononic)
c) Calculate the thermal time constant for the wire to thermalize
d) If a 100 ns microwave pulse deposits 0.1 pJ of energy in the qubit, estimate the temperature spike
e) How long until the system returns to within 0.5 mK of base temperature?

Use: Gold thermal conductivity at 20 mK ~ 10 W/m/K, density = 19,300 kg/m^3, Debye temperature = 170 K, electronic heat capacity coefficient gamma = 0.73 mJ/mol/K^2, molar mass = 197 g/mol.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

print("Gold Wire Bond Thermalization Analysis")
print("="*60)

# Wire parameters
d = 25e-6      # 25 um diameter in meters
L = 2e-3       # 2 mm length in meters

# Gold properties
rho = 19300    # kg/m^3
kappa = 10     # W/m/K at 20 mK (approximate)
Theta_D = 170  # K, Debye temperature
gamma = 0.73e-3  # J/mol/K^2, electronic heat capacity coefficient
M = 197e-3     # kg/mol, molar mass

# Operating conditions
T_base = 0.020  # K

# Part a) Thermal resistance
A = np.pi * (d/2)**2
R_thermal = # Your calculation
print(f"\na) Thermal resistance: {R_thermal:.1f} K/W")

# Part b) Heat capacity
# Electronic: C_e = gamma * T * n_moles
# Phononic: C_ph = (12/5) * pi^4 * R * n_moles * (T/Theta_D)^3
V = A * L
mass = rho * V
n_moles = mass / M

C_electronic = # Your calculation
C_phononic = # Your calculation
C_total = # Your calculation

print(f"\nb) Heat capacities at {T_base*1000:.0f} mK:")
print(f"   Electronic: {C_electronic:.2e} J/K")
print(f"   Phononic: {C_phononic:.2e} J/K")
print(f"   Total: {C_total:.2e} J/K")

# Part c) Thermal time constant
tau = # Your calculation
print(f"\nc) Thermal time constant: {tau*1e6:.2f} us")

# Part d) Temperature spike from pulse
E_pulse = 0.1e-12  # 0.1 pJ in Joules
dT_spike = # Your calculation
T_peak = # Your calculation
print(f"\nd) For 0.1 pJ pulse:")
print(f"   Temperature spike: {dT_spike*1000:.2f} mK")
print(f"   Peak temperature: {T_peak*1000:.2f} mK")

# Part e) Return time
# T(t) = T_base + dT_spike * exp(-t/tau)
# Solve for t when T - T_base = 0.5 mK
dT_final = 0.5e-3  # 0.5 mK
t_return = # Your calculation
print(f"\ne) Time to return within 0.5 mK: {t_return*1e6:.2f} us")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

print("Gold Wire Bond Thermalization Analysis")
print("="*60)

# Wire parameters
d = 25e-6      # 25 um diameter in meters
L = 2e-3       # 2 mm length in meters

# Gold properties
rho = 19300    # kg/m^3
kappa = 10     # W/m/K at 20 mK
Theta_D = 170  # K, Debye temperature
gamma = 0.73e-3  # J/mol/K^2
M = 197e-3     # kg/mol
R_gas = 8.314  # J/mol/K

# Operating conditions
T_base = 0.020  # K

print(f"\nWire parameters:")
print(f"  Diameter: {d*1e6:.0f} um")
print(f"  Length: {L*1e3:.0f} mm")
print(f"  Base temperature: {T_base*1000:.0f} mK")

# Part a) Thermal resistance
A = np.pi * (d/2)**2
R_thermal = L / (kappa * A)
print(f"\na) Thermal resistance:")
print(f"   Cross-sectional area: {A*1e12:.2f} um²")
print(f"   R_thermal = L/(kappa*A) = {R_thermal:.1f} K/W")

# Part b) Heat capacity
V = A * L
mass = rho * V
n_moles = mass / M

print(f"\nb) Heat capacity calculation:")
print(f"   Volume: {V*1e18:.2f} um³")
print(f"   Mass: {mass*1e12:.3f} pg")
print(f"   Moles: {n_moles:.2e} mol")

# Electronic heat capacity: C_e = gamma * T * n_moles
C_electronic = gamma * T_base * n_moles

# Phononic heat capacity (Debye model at low T):
# C_ph = (12/5) * pi^4 * R * n_moles * (T/Theta_D)^3
C_phononic = (12/5) * np.pi**4 * R_gas * n_moles * (T_base/Theta_D)**3

C_total = C_electronic + C_phononic

print(f"\n   At {T_base*1000:.0f} mK:")
print(f"   Electronic: C_e = gamma*T*n = {C_electronic:.2e} J/K")
print(f"   Phononic: C_ph = (12pi^4/5)*R*n*(T/Theta_D)^3 = {C_phononic:.2e} J/K")
print(f"   Total: {C_total:.2e} J/K")
print(f"   Dominant contribution: {'Electronic' if C_electronic > C_phononic else 'Phononic'}")

# Part c) Thermal time constant
# tau = R_thermal * C_total
tau = R_thermal * C_total
print(f"\nc) Thermal time constant:")
print(f"   tau = R * C = {tau:.2e} s")
print(f"   tau = {tau*1e6:.2f} us")

# Part d) Temperature spike from pulse
E_pulse = 0.1e-12  # 0.1 pJ

# Instantaneous temperature rise assuming adiabatic absorption
dT_spike = E_pulse / C_total
T_peak = T_base + dT_spike

print(f"\nd) Temperature spike from 0.1 pJ pulse:")
print(f"   Energy: {E_pulse*1e12:.1f} pJ")
print(f"   dT = E/C = {dT_spike:.2e} K = {dT_spike*1000:.2f} mK")
print(f"   Peak temperature: {T_peak*1000:.2f} mK")

# Part e) Return time
# Exponential decay: T(t) = T_base + dT_spike * exp(-t/tau)
# Want: dT_spike * exp(-t/tau) = dT_final
# t = -tau * ln(dT_final / dT_spike)
dT_final = 0.5e-3  # 0.5 mK
t_return = -tau * np.log(dT_final / dT_spike)

print(f"\ne) Time to return within 0.5 mK of base:")
print(f"   Solving: {dT_spike*1000:.2f}*exp(-t/tau) = {dT_final*1000:.1f} mK")
print(f"   t = tau * ln(dT_spike/dT_final) = {t_return:.2e} s")
print(f"   t = {t_return*1e6:.2f} us")

# Additional analysis: plot temperature evolution
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

t_range = np.linspace(0, 5*tau, 200)
T_t = T_base + dT_spike * np.exp(-t_range / tau)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(t_range * 1e6, T_t * 1000, 'b-', linewidth=2)
plt.axhline(y=T_base*1000, color='g', linestyle='--', label='Base temperature')
plt.axhline(y=(T_base + dT_final)*1000, color='r', linestyle='--', label='Target (base + 0.5 mK)')
plt.axvline(x=t_return*1e6, color='k', linestyle=':', alpha=0.5)
plt.xlabel('Time (us)')
plt.ylabel('Temperature (mK)')
plt.title('Wire Bond Thermalization After Pulse')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.xlim(0, t_range[-1]*1e6)
plt.show()

# Summary for system design
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("Design Implications:")
print("="*60)
print(f"1. Thermalization time ({tau*1e6:.1f} us) is much longer than pulse (100 ns)")
print(f"   -> Heat is deposited faster than it can dissipate")
print(f"\n2. Temperature spike ({dT_spike*1000:.1f} mK) is significant")
print(f"   -> May affect qubit coherence during recovery")
print(f"\n3. Recovery time ({t_return*1e6:.1f} us) sets minimum repetition interval")
print(f"   -> For continuous operation, consider duty cycle limits")
print(f"\n4. To reduce spike, options include:")
print(f"   - Use multiple wire bonds (parallel thermal paths)")
print(f"   - Increase wire cross-section")
print(f"   - Improve thermal contact at bond pads")
hints: Thermal resistance of wire: R = L/(kappa*A)
Electronic heat capacity: C_e = gamma * T * n_moles
Phononic heat capacity: C_ph = (12/5) * pi^4 * R * n * (T/Theta_D)^3
